evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI/APB/MMIO requester reaching AES through ariane_peripherals.aes -> axi2apb_64_32 -> apb_to_reg -> aes_wrapper.external_bus_io
evidence_slots.expected_guard: reads of AES key material and internal intermediate state should be blocked or require an explicit privileged/debug-authorized mode; normal MMIO/APB reads should expose only non-secret status/ciphertext as intended
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: aes_wrapper drives external_bus_io.rdata with key_big slices and inter_state slices based only on external_bus_io.addr[6:2]. key_big is directly assigned from key_in. external_bus_io.ready is tied high and error tied low. No local privilege, authorization, debug, lock, or read-disable guard is present in the wrapper read mux. ariane_peripherals connects an AXI AES port through axi2apb and apb_to_reg into this REG_BUS interface.
evidence_slots.path: AXI master -> axi_node_intf_wrap master[ariane_soc::AES] -> ariane_peripherals.aes AXI port -> i_axi2apb_64_32_aes -> i_apb_to_reg_aes -> reg_bus_aes -> aes_wrapper.external_bus_io -> read mux -> aes_prdata/aes.r_data
evidence_slots.state_condition: For external_bus_io reads selecting addr[6:2] values corresponding to the key and inter_state cases, rdata returns key_big[191:0] and inter_state[127:0] in 32-bit slices. key_in is live AES key input; inter_state is AES intermediate output from aes_192_sed. No ct_valid, start, busy, reset, privilege, or debug state condition is checked by the read mux.
evidence_slots.impact: A software-visible MMIO requester that can reach the AES window can disclose AES key material and internal AES intermediate state.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI/APB/MMIO requester reaching AES
evidence_slots.object: AES plaintext/control/state registers p_c, state, start
evidence_slots.expected_guard: writes that configure cryptographic operation inputs/control should be constrained by valid address decode and any required privilege/lock policy for the AES peripheral; write strobes should not permit unintended update aliases
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: On reset aes_wrapper clears start, p_c, and state. After reset, if external_bus_io.write is asserted, addr[6:2] case values update start, p_c[3:0], and state[3:0]. There is address decode, but no local privilege/authorization check, no lock guard, and no busy/active-operation guard in the visible wrapper. Upstream fabric has a privilege connectivity map sourced from ROM2 access-control registers, but the intended policy for AES configuration writes and its complete enforcement cannot be concluded solely from the provided observations.
evidence_slots.path: AXI master -> axi_node_intf_wrap -> ariane_peripherals.aes -> axi2apb_64_32_aes -> apb_to_reg_aes -> aes_wrapper.external_bus_io.write -> p_c/state/start -> aes_192_sed
evidence_slots.state_condition: On ~rst_ni start, p_c, and state are cleared. With external_bus_io.write high, addr[6:2]==0 updates start from wdata[0], 1-4 update p_c words, and 16-19 update state words. No start/ct_valid/busy condition is checked for writes during active AES operation.
evidence_slots.impact: If AES operation control or plaintext/state programming is intended to be privileged, locked, or frozen while active, the local wrapper does not enforce that policy; however upstream access-control may mediate window reachability.

evidence_slots.observed_behavior: bootrom captures raddr_q on req_i && !we_i and writes mem[addr-derived index] <= wdata_i on req_i && we_i. rdata_o is continuously assigned from mem[raddr_q] when in range, otherwise zero. The testharness connects axi2mem outputs rom_req, rom_we, rom_addr, rom_wdata directly to bootrom ports.

evidence_slots.impact: A requester able to issue writes to the ROM window can modify boot ROM contents and potentially alter boot code or security initialization.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester in ariane_peripherals through ROM2_FUSE window
evidence_slots.object: rom2 secure_reg contents, AES/JTAG/access-control key material, and key-location/access-control outputs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: ROM2 contents and any key-location output should be read-only or access-controlled; writes should be rejected/ignored and reads of key-related locations should require intended privilege/authorization or be non-secret by design
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rom2 initializes secure_reg from const mem on reset. If req_i is asserted and we_i is 0, it captures raddr_q; if req_i and we_i are 1, it writes secure_reg[index] <= wdata_i. rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q] when in range. ariane_peripherals connects ROM2 through axi2mem to the rom2_fuse AXI port and derives jtag_key and access_ctrl_reg from key_reg_out/secure_reg. No local privilege or write-protect guard is visible in rom2.
evidence_slots.path: AXI requester -> ariane_peripherals.rom2_fuse AXI port -> axi2mem i_axi2rom2 -> rom2_req/rom2_we/rom2_addr/rom2_wdata -> rom2.secure_reg/rdata_o -> rom2_rdata plus key_reg_out -> jtag_key/access_ctrl_reg
evidence_slots.state_condition: After reset, secure_reg contains const ROM2/fuse entries including AES and JTAG key values and access-control values. With rom2_req asserted and rom2_we=0, raddr_q selects a readable entry. With rom2_req asserted and rom2_we=1, secure_reg at the addressed entry is overwritten. rdata_o is continuously driven from selected secure_reg entry if in range.
evidence_slots.impact: An MMIO requester reaching ROM2 can read key material and can modify jtag_key/access-control policy state, potentially unlocking debug or changing fabric permissions.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible APB/MMIO requester
evidence_slots.object: PLIC, packet, AES, UART, ROM2 APB/AXI-visible register windows
evidence_slots.expected_guard: security-sensitive peripheral configuration registers should be reachable only through intended address windows and privilege policy; APB decode must not alias protected registers across peripherals or ignore psel/pwrite/penable
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane_peripherals has distinct AXI input ports for plic, aes, rom2_fuse, pkt, uart, spi, and ethernet. PLIC, PKT, AES, and UART use separate axi2apb_64_32 bridges and apb_to_reg/APB peripherals; ROM2 uses a separate axi2mem bridge. AES APB path has its own aes_penable/pwrite/paddr/psel/prdata signals. Address window base/length values are provided to axi_node_intf_wrap in the testharness. However, the full psel generation, prdata muxing, and all peripheral address windows are not fully visible, and the upstream axi_node route policy is not fully proven.
evidence_slots.path: AXI fabric address decode -> ariane_peripherals per-peripheral AXI port -> separate axi2apb_64_32 bridges for APB peripherals or axi2mem for ROM2 -> peripheral register interfaces
evidence_slots.state_condition: Potential alias conditions involve overlapping base/range values in start_addr_i/end_addr_i, multiple selected peripheral ports, pwrite without psel/penable, or default prdata behavior. The observations show separate ports and bridge instances but not enough full decode behavior to rule out all aliasing.
evidence_slots.impact: If address ranges overlap or psel/prdata/write-enable logic aliases, software could reach sensitive AES/ROM2/PKT/PLIC registers through unintended windows. Current evidence does not prove such aliasing nor fully rule it out.

ROM2_FUSE has a separate axi2mem path to rom2.
PKT has a separate axi2apb path (partially observed).
AES has a separate axi2apb and apb_to_reg path with aes_* APB signals.
peripheral base and length constants define the intended windows.
start/end address vectors include Debug, ROM, ROM2, PKT, CLINT, PLIC, UART, AES, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, DRAM.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need full untruncated ariane_peripherals APB bridge blocks, prdata mux/default behavior, and axi_node decode behavior to confirm no overlapping range or simultaneous selection.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: APB windows for AES/PLIC/PKT/UART and AXI memory window for ROM2 are separately instantiated, but full address decode/psel/prdata mux behavior and alias-freedom across all windows cannot be proven from supplied excerpts.
inspection_records.notes: No direct candidate aliasing evidence was provided; main confirmed issues are sensitive contents behind intended windows.

evidence_slots.subject: debug/JTAG reset requester or external reset source
evidence_slots.object: AES key/intermediate/control state, debug authorization state, ROM/peripheral security state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug-triggered reset should not leave security-sensitive state uncleared or force a mode transition that bypasses locks/authorization; reset sources should be authorized and clear key/intermediate/debug state as required
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_top outputs ndmreset, which is combined with rst_ni in rstgen to produce ndmreset_n for core/fabric reset paths. No jtag_key/authentication guard is visible before ndmreset_o. aes_wrapper reset clears start, p_c, and state, but key_big is combinationally assigned from key_in and not locally cleared; key_in is driven from ROM2 key_reg_out[0] in ariane_peripherals. aes_wrapper read mux continues to expose key_big and inter_state slices without guard. ROM2 reloads secure_reg from const mem on reset, so key material is restored rather than scrubbed.
evidence_slots.path: DMI/JTAG/debug request -> dm_top.ndmreset_o -> rstgen rst_ni & ~ndmreset -> ndmreset_n distributed to core/fabric; ROM2 reset reloads secure_reg; AES key_in/key_big and inter_state remain readable via AES MMIO read mux
evidence_slots.state_condition: When ndmreset is asserted, rstgen input reset is forced. aes_wrapper reset clears control/plaintext/state registers, but key_big follows key_in continuously. ROM2 reset loads secure_reg from mem. After reset release, APB/MMIO reads of AES key/inter_state offsets are still unguarded; inter_state reset behavior inside aes_192_sed is not fully evidenced.
evidence_slots.impact: An unauthenticated debug reset can cause security-relevant mode transitions and does not scrub key material from the externally readable AES path. Post-reset software-visible reads may still disclose key material; debug reset authorization is not evidenced.

rtl_evidence.evidence: rstgen uses rst_ni & (~ndmreset) and outputs ndmreset_n.
dm_top outputs ndmreset and debug_req_core; no visible authorization guard in the harness excerpt.
AES wrapper reset clears start, p_c, and state.
key_big is continuously assigned from key_in, not reset locally.
AES read mux exposes key_big and inter_state slices without reset/authorization gating.
ROM2 secure_reg is loaded from const mem on reset.
ROM2 secure_reg key_reg_out feeds jtag_key and access_ctrl_reg; AES key input is noted in observations as driven from key_reg_out[0].

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need aes_192_sed reset behavior for inter_state and full debug authentication policy to determine exact post-reset residue beyond key_in/key_big exposure.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Debug ndmreset is driven from dm_top without visible authorization gating at the harness, and AES secret key input/intermediate read mux exposure can persist or reappear after reset because key material is supplied from ROM2 secure_reg and read access is unguarded.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate finding combines debug reset authorization absence with confirmed lack of key scrubbing/read gating in AES/ROM2 integration.

2:  * ROM2: Which have all the keys.

5: module rom2 (
6:    input  logic         clk_i,
7:    input  logic         rst_ni,
8:    input  logic         req_i,
9:    input  logic         we_i,
10:    input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
11:    input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,
12:    output logic [63:0]  rdata_o,
13:    output logic [3:0][191:0]  secure_reg // Ideally [1:0] should be [RomSize-1:0]. In this case RomSize is 2.
14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
